name: Pupukea Tide Pools
tagline: Sheltered tide pools perfect for a mellow snorkel right next to Shark's Cove.
parking: Free lot across from Pupukea Beach Park (shared with Shark's Cove); fills by 9am in summer
bestTime: Summer only (May–Sep); low tide reveals the best pools; mornings are calmest
talkStory: Pupukea means 'white shell hill' and this coastline is part of the Pupukea Marine Life Conservation District, established in 1983 to protect the reef from collection and fishing. The tide pools trap juvenile humuhumunukunukuapuaa, hermit crabs, and bright-red slate-pencil urchins in shallow bowls perfect for kids. Experienced snorkelers work the outer pools at low tide where octopus hide in crevices and endemic cleaner wrasse run cleaning stations.
